source_paper_title: Nonregular McKean–Vlasov Equations and Calibration Problem in Local Stochastic Volatility Models
context:

However, we are unable to have a more general dependency for the coefficients \(\lambda\) and \(\beta\). Also, we cannot provide a uniqueness result for this system.
title: Uniqueness for non-regular McKean--Vlasov SDE (2.2) under Assumption 2.1
background_latex: Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,(\mathcal F_t)_{t\ge 0},\mathbb P)\) support two one-dimensional Brownian motions \((W_t)_{t\ge 0}\) and \((B_t)_{t\ge 0}\) with \(d\langle W,B\rangle_t=\theta\,dt\) for some \(\theta\in(-1,1)\). Let \(T>0\). Let \(p_0:\mathbb R^2\to\mathbb R_+\) be a probability density. For any density \(f\in L^1([0,T]\times\mathbb R^2)\) with \(f\ge 0\), define Borel coefficients \(b(f):[0,T]\times\mathbb R^2\to\mathbb R\) and \(\sigma(f):[0,T]\times\mathbb R^2\to\mathbb R\), and Borel maps \(\lambda,\beta:[0,T]\times\mathbb R\to\mathbb R\). Consider an \(\mathbb R^2\)-valued adapted continuous process \(X_t=(X_t^1,X_t^2)\) with law \(\mathcal L(X_t)(dx)=p(t,x)\,dx\), where \(p(t,\cdot)\) is a density for each \(t\in[0,T]\). The dynamics are \[dX_t^1 = b(p)(t,X_t)\,dt + \sigma(p)(t,X_t)\,dW_t,\qquad dX_t^2 = \lambda(t,X_t^2)\,dt + \beta(t,X_t^2)\,dB_t.\] Assume the coefficients satisfy the paper's Assumption 2.1 (bounded drift, uniformly non-degenerate diffusion matrix built from \(\sigma(p)\) and \(\beta\), and a weak continuity condition of \(b(p)\) and the diffusion matrix in \(p\) under \(L^1\)-convergence).
problem_definition_latex: Problem: Prove (or disprove) uniqueness (in law, and ideally pathwise/strong uniqueness) for solutions \((X_t)_{t\in[0,T]}\) of the McKean--Vlasov SDE \[dX_t^1 = b(p)(t,X_t)\,dt + \sigma(p)(t,X_t)\,dW_t,\qquad dX_t^2 = \lambda(t,X_t^2)\,dt + \beta(t,X_t^2)\,dB_t,\] where \(p(t,\cdot)\) is the density of \(\mathcal L(X_t)\) and \(\mathcal L(X_0)(dx)=p_0(x)\,dx\), under Assumption 2.1 on \((b,\sigma,\lambda,\beta)\) and the stated regularity/integrability assumptions on \(p_0\).
latex:
Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,(\mathcal F_t)_{t\ge 0},\mathbb P)\) support two one-dimensional Brownian motions \((W_t)_{t\ge 0}\) and \((B_t)_{t\ge 0}\) with \(d\langle W,B\rangle_t=\theta\,dt\) for some \(\theta\in(-1,1)\). Let \(T>0\). Let \(p_0:\mathbb R^2\to\mathbb R_+\) be a probability density. For any density \(f\in L^1([0,T]\times\mathbb R^2)\) with \(f\ge 0\), define Borel coefficients \(b(f):[0,T]\times\mathbb R^2\to\mathbb R\) and \(\sigma(f):[0,T]\times\mathbb R^2\to\mathbb R\), and Borel maps \(\lambda,\beta:[0,T]\times\mathbb R\to\mathbb R\). Consider an \(\mathbb R^2\)-valued adapted continuous process \(X_t=(X_t^1,X_t^2)\) with law \(\mathcal L(X_t)(dx)=p(t,x)\,dx\), where \(p(t,\cdot)\) is a density for each \(t\in[0,T]\). The dynamics are \[dX_t^1 = b(p)(t,X_t)\,dt + \sigma(p)(t,X_t)\,dW_t,\qquad dX_t^2 = \lambda(t,X_t^2)\,dt + \beta(t,X_t^2)\,dB_t.\] Assume the coefficients satisfy the paper's Assumption 2.1 (bounded drift, uniformly non-degenerate diffusion matrix built from \(\sigma(p)\) and \(\beta\), and a weak continuity condition of \(b(p)\) and the diffusion matrix in \(p\) under \(L^1\)-convergence).

Problem:
Problem: Prove (or disprove) uniqueness (in law, and ideally pathwise/strong uniqueness) for solutions \((X_t)_{t\in[0,T]}\) of the McKean--Vlasov SDE \[dX_t^1 = b(p)(t,X_t)\,dt + \sigma(p)(t,X_t)\,dW_t,\qquad dX_t^2 = \lambda(t,X_t^2)\,dt + \beta(t,X_t^2)\,dB_t,\] where \(p(t,\cdot)\) is the density of \(\mathcal L(X_t)\) and \(\mathcal L(X_0)(dx)=p_0(x)\,dx\), under Assumption 2.1 on \((b,\sigma,\lambda,\beta)\) and the stated regularity/integrability assumptions on \(p_0\).
location_in_paper: Section 2 (Setup and Main results), Remark 2.4(iii), page 5.
open_problem_quote: However, we are unable to have a more general dependency for the coefficients \(\lambda\) and \(\beta\). Also, we cannot provide a uniqueness result for this system.
